title: Peer Support & Mentoring for Men
description: Peer mentoring and case management for men in early recovery, plus community-building recovery retreats. One-on-one support with basic needs and legal referrals, from an organization run by men with lived recovery experience since 1996.
engage: Email thejmir09crew@gmail.com to connect with a peer mentor or learn about upcoming recovery retreats. Located at 12604 SE Stark St, Portland.
content:
Services

  • Peer Support & Case Management: One-on-one mentoring with help for basic needs and legal referrals
  • Recovery Retreats: Two ~3-day retreats a year with fellowship, skill-building, and connection
  • Annual Toy Drive: Holiday gifts for children of men in treatment

Who It's For

Men in early recovery who benefit from peer connection and mentoring.

title: Black Peer-Led Recovery Center
description: A Black, peer-led recovery center offering certified recovery mentors, recovery meetings, exercise passes, and a welcoming community — centering Black, Indigenous, and People of Color while open to anyone seeking recovery or harm reduction. Open 9am–6pm, Monday–Saturday.
engage: Drop in at 4200 NE MLK Jr Blvd (9am–6pm, Mon–Sat), call 503-249-8559, or email info@miraclesrecovery.org. Additional locations in NE Portland and Hillsboro.
content:
Services

  • Peer Support: Certified Recovery Mentors with lived experience
  • Recovery Meetings: Regular gatherings in a safe, recovery-focused space
  • Exercise Passes and wellness access
  • Membership & community programs

Who It's For

Anyone seeking recovery or harm reduction, with particular emphasis on serving Black, Indigenous, and People of Color communities.

Locations

Main: 4200 NE MLK Jr Blvd. Also Miracles Central (1306 NE 2nd Ave) and Washington County (4660 NE Belknap Ct, Hillsboro).
street_address: 4200 NE Martin Luther King Jr Blvd

title: Sober Housing
description: Recovery-focused sober housing through the Miracles Club, providing a safe, substance-free place to live within a Black, peer-led recovery community.
engage: Call 503-249-8559 or email info@miraclesrecovery.org to ask about sober housing availability.
content:
Housing

The Miracles Club offers residential sober-living support as part of its recovery community, giving people a stable, substance-free environment to sustain recovery.

Who It's For

People in recovery seeking sober housing, centering BIPOC communities.
street_address: 4200 NE Martin Luther King Jr Blvd

title: Veteran Peer Support
description: Free one-on-one peer support for veterans and active/prior military service members living with mental health conditions, delivered by peers with shared military experience.
engage: Call 503-228-5692 or email info@namimultnomah.org to connect with a veteran peer supporter. By appointment.
content:
Program

Peer-based mental health support specifically for veterans and current/former service members — connection, resource navigation, and guidance from someone who understands military experience.

Who It's For

Veterans and active/prior military service members living with mental health conditions.
modes: In Person; Online; By Appointment Only

title: Wraparound Family Support
description: Family Partners work alongside families and service providers to build customized plans that address a child's specific mental health needs — support from people with lived family experience.
engage: Call 503-228-5692 or email info@namimultnomah.org to learn about Wraparound family support. By appointment.
content:
Program

Wraparound pairs families with a Family Partner who helps coordinate care and develop a personalized plan around a child's mental health needs, in collaboration with providers.

Who It's For

Children with mental health needs and their families.
modes: In Person; Online; By Appointment Only

title: Mental Health Clinical Care
description: Outpatient and residential mental health treatment tailored to each person, with usually same-day or next-day intake. Part of New Narrative's continuum helping people build tools to thrive in the community.
engage: Call the intake team at 503-726-3742 or email intake@newnarrativepdx.org — same-day or next-day appointments are often available.
content:
Program

Individualized outpatient and residential mental health treatment, delivered across Portland-area clinics (including Spring Creek in Tigard and Mill Park in Portland).

Who It's For

People in the greater Portland community living with mental health conditions.

How to Access

Contact the intake team at 503-726-3742 or intake@newnarrativepdx.org.

title: Peer Programs
description: Peer-to-peer mental health support from people with lived experience, helping participants build connection, skills, and their own path to independence.
engage: Call 503-726-3690 or visit newnarrativepdx.org/get-help-now to connect with peer programs.
content:
Program

Peer support services that pair participants with people who have lived mental health experience, focused on connection, empowerment, and community integration.

Who It's For

People living with mental health conditions in the greater Portland area.

title: Supportive Housing
description: Transitional and permanent housing integrated with mental health services, giving people living with mental illness a stable place to live plus the support to maintain it.
engage: Call 503-726-3690 or visit newnarrativepdx.org/get-help-now to ask about housing.
content:
Program

Housing options — transitional and permanent supportive housing — combined with mental health support to help people stabilize and build independence.

Who It's For

People living with mental health conditions who need stable, supportive housing.

title: Free Legal Information & Aid Finder
description: A free statewide website that explains civil legal issues in plain language — housing, family law, public benefits, and more — with step-by-step guides and a tool to find free/low-cost legal aid and lawyer referrals. Available in English, Spanish, Chinese, and Vietnamese.
engage: Visit oregonlawhelp.org to read legal guides, use the guided help tool, or find a legal-aid organization near you. Free to use.
content:
What It Offers

  • Legal Information Library: plain-language guides organized by topic with step-by-step instructions
  • Find Legal Help: connections to free/low-cost legal aid and Oregon State Bar lawyer referrals
  • Guided Navigation: a tool to help you figure out where to start

Topics

Housing/eviction, family law, public benefits, and other civil legal matters.

Note

This is a free information resource written by Oregon attorneys — educational material, not personal legal advice. Available in English, Spanish, Mandarin Chinese, and Vietnamese.

title: Recovery Housing Directory
description: A free statewide online registry of recovery (sober) housing in Oregon. Search certified recovery homes by length of stay — long-term, transitional, or emergency beds — and see availability, pricing, and homes serving LGBTQ+ and culturally-specific communities.
engage: Search available recovery homes for free at oregonrecoveryresidences.org. Housing providers can also list residences by creating a free account.
content:
What It Offers

A searchable, free directory of recovery residences across Oregon, maintained by residence operators. Filter by length of stay:

  • Long-term housing (months to years)
  • Transitional housing (months to ~2 years)
  • Emergency beds (short-term)

Listings show available houses, bed counts, pricing, and homes serving LGBTQ+ and culturally-specific communities.

Who It's For

People seeking recovery housing, and the agencies/operators who list it.

title: Medical Clinic (FQHC)
description: Primary medical care for people who are unhoused or have limited access to healthcare, at Outside In's downtown Portland clinic. Low-barrier, welcoming care from an organization serving vulnerable Portlanders since 1968.
engage: Call 503-535-3860 or visit the clinic at 1132 SW 13th Ave, Portland. General line: 503-535-3800.
content:
Services

Primary and preventive medical care, including an on-site pharmacy, for unhoused and underserved individuals.

Who It's For

People experiencing homelessness or with limited access to healthcare.

title: Behavioral Health Services
description: Mental health and counseling support integrated with Outside In's medical and social services for unhoused and underserved people.
engage: Call 503-535-3800 or visit 1132 SW 13th Ave, Portland, to access behavioral health services.
content:
Services

Mental health counseling and psychiatric support, offered alongside medical care and harm-reduction services.

Who It's For

People needing accessible mental and behavioral health care, including those experiencing homelessness.

title: Harm Reduction & Substance User Engagement
description: Harm-reduction services and outreach for people who use drugs — supplies, engagement, and connection to care, meeting people where they are without judgment. Locations in downtown Portland, Clackamas, and Oregon City.
engage: Call 503-535-3826 or visit 1219 SW Main St, Portland (also Clackamas and Oregon City).
content:
Services

Harm-reduction supplies and support, outreach, and low-barrier connection to medical and behavioral health care for people who use drugs.

Locations

  • Downtown Portland: 1219 SW Main St
  • Clackamas: 8800 SE 80th Ave
  • Oregon City: 700 Molalla Ave

Who It's For

People who use drugs — no abstinence required.

title: Young Adult Services
description: Comprehensive support for young adults experiencing homelessness — help with housing, meals, mentorship, and connection to Outside In's health and social services.
engage: Call 503-535-3800 or visit outsidein.org/young-adult-services to connect a young person to services.
content:
Services

Wraparound support for homeless young adults, including housing assistance, meals, mentorship, and access to medical and behavioral health care.

Who It's For

Young adults experiencing homelessness.

title: Gender-Affirming Hormone Therapy
description: Clinically-indicated hormone therapy and affirming health care for transgender and gender-diverse people, provided through Outside In's medical clinic.
engage: Call 503-535-3860 or visit 1132 SW 13th Ave, Portland, to access gender-affirming care.
content:
Services

Clinically-indicated gender-affirming hormone therapy and related supportive health care in a welcoming, affirming setting.

Who It's For

Transgender and gender-diverse individuals.

title: Native-Centered Recovery Support
description: Culturally-specific recovery support centering Native Americans (open to all) — drop-in recovery centers with Native-based AA/NA and 'wellbriety' meetings, skill-building groups, recovery events, and culture nights for Native families. Centers in Portland, Salem, Roseburg, and Medford, open daily 2pm–10pm.
engage: Drop in at the Portland center (Spence Community Center, 10209 SE Division St) daily 2–10pm, or call 971-205-5144. Admin: 971-509-2391.
content:
Services

  • Native-based AA, NA, and wellbriety meetings
  • Recovery events and skill-building groups
  • Culture nights for Native American families
  • Drop-in recovery center space

Locations (open daily 2pm–10pm)

  • Portland: Spence Community Center, 10209 SE Division St — 971-205-5144
  • Salem: BuffaloBoy Community Center, 1800 State St — 971-719-2155
  • Roseburg: Chief Barrett Community Center, 1941 NE Stephens St — 541-637-0216
  • Medford: Archambault Community Center, 1014 N Riverside Ave — 458-226-2120

Who It's For

Anyone seeking recovery, with a focus on Native Americans and culturally-centered programming.

title: Family Village Shelter
description: A year-round shelter for families with children experiencing homelessness, with private sleeping spaces, food, showers, laundry, computers, and clothing — supported by hundreds of volunteers.
engage: Call 211 and ask about Family Shelter to access Path Home's Family Village.
content:
Services

Safe, year-round family shelter offering private sleeping spaces, meals, showers, laundry, computer access, and clothing while families work toward permanent housing.

Who It's For

Families with children experiencing homelessness.

How to Access

Call 211 and ask about Family Shelter.

title: Rapid Rehousing & Homelessness Prevention
description: Housing First rapid rehousing (move families into housing quickly with 6–12 months of rent assistance and case management), plus prevention aid — security deposits, moving costs, back rent within 72 hours — and unconditional cash assistance.
engage: Call 211 and ask about family housing and prevention services, or visit path-home.org.
content:
Services

  • Housing First / Rapid Rehousing: quick move-in with rent assistance and case management for 6–12 months
  • Prevention: deposits, moving costs, and back rent for families facing eviction — often within 72 hours
  • Cash Transfer: unconditional cash assistance to stabilize families

Who It's For

Families with children experiencing or at risk of homelessness (about 600 families a year).

title: Medical Detox (Withdrawal Management)
description: An 8-day non-hospital medical detox in a home-like setting, with medication-assisted treatment to manage withdrawal from opioids, benzodiazepines, alcohol, cocaine, meth, marijuana, and prescription painkillers. Accepts many insurance plans (not OHP).
engage: Call (855) 770-0577 for a confidential assessment and insurance verification. Located at 10600 SE McLoughlin Blvd, Suite 207, Milwaukie.
content:
Program

Medically-supervised 8-day detox in a comfortable, home-like environment, using medication-assisted treatment where appropriate to ease withdrawal safely.

Substances Treated

Opioids, benzodiazepines, alcohol, cocaine, methamphetamine, marijuana, and prescription painkillers.

Insurance

In-network with Aetna, Regence, First Health, Moda, Providence, Tricare, TriWest, Healthnet, and Beacon. Does not accept OHP at this time.

title: Outpatient & Day Treatment (PHP/IOP)
description: Continued addiction treatment after detox — Partial Hospitalization (PHP), Intensive Outpatient (IOP), and standard outpatient programs, with individual and group therapy, gender-specific tracks, DUII treatment, and alumni aftercare.
engage: Call (855) 770-0577 to arrange treatment and verify insurance.
content:
Programs

  • Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP)
  • Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)
  • Standard outpatient
  • Individual and group therapy
  • Gender-specific programs (men's and women's)
  • DUII treatment
  • Aftercare and alumni services

Who It's For

People continuing addiction recovery after detox, including those with co-occurring mental health conditions.
